authors: Al-Kaisy A, Palmisani S, Smith TE, Pang D, Lam K, Burgoyne W, Houghton R, Hudson E, Lucas J
title: 10 kHz High-Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation for Chronic Axial Low Back Pain in Patients With No History of Spinal Surgery: A Preliminary, Prospective, Open Label and Proof-of-Concept Study
study_question: Is 10 kHz high-frequency spinal cord stimulation effective for chronic axial low back pain in surgically-naive patients?
population_assessed: Chronic low back pain patients without prior spinal surgery, medically refractory, not candidates for surgical intervention.
outcome_measures: Back pain VAS, leg pain VAS, ODI, EQ-5D, SF-36, opioid use, quality of life, employment status
inclusion_criteria: Age 18-65, axial low back pain ≥6 months, VAS ≥5, failed conservative treatment, no prior spinal surgery, degenerative disc disease confirmed.
answer_to_study_question: HF10 therapy significantly reduced back pain by 73%, ODI by 48%, opioid use by 64%, improved quality of life and employment at 12 months.
